title: Fred Burggraf Award
eligibilityNote: Author must be 35 or younger; outstanding paper published in the Transportation Research Record. No separate application—Annual Meeting/TRR papers are considered automatically.
prize: Presented at the TRB Annual Meeting (est. 1966)

title: Pyke Johnson Award
eligibilityNote: Outstanding paper in planning and environment published in the TRR; open to any author. No separate nomination.
prize: Presented at the TRB Annual Meeting

title: D. Grant Mickle Award
eligibilityNote: Outstanding paper in operation, safety, and maintenance of transportation facilities published in the TRR (est. 1976); open to any author.
prize: Presented at the TRB Annual Meeting

title: Charley V. Wootan Award
eligibilityNote: Outstanding paper in policy and organization published in the TRR (est. 2004); open to any author.
prize: Presented at the TRB Annual Meeting

title: K. B. Woods Award
eligibilityNote: Outstanding paper in design and construction of transportation facilities published in the TRR (est. 1971); open to any author.
prize: Presented at the TRB Annual Meeting

title: TSL Dissertation Prize
eligibilityNote: PhD dissertation in transportation science and logistics completed/submitted between June 1 of the prior year and May 31 of the award year. Oldest such prize (since 1970).

title: TSL Best Paper Award
eligibilityNote: Outstanding paper in a refereed journal presenting innovative OR/quantitative approaches to transportation/logistics; open to any author.

title: TSL Best Student Paper Award
eligibilityNote: Paper primarily authored by a student, broadly of interest to the TSL community.
prize: $500 honorarium + fast-track review at Transportation Science

title: Stella Dafermos Award (Mid-Career)
eligibilityNote: Mid-career scholar 10-20 years past PhD (as of Dec 31 of nomination year) with fundamental contributions to TSL. At most one recipient/year (since 2022).

title: Robert Herman Lifetime Achievement Award
eligibilityNote: Fundamental, sustained career-long contributions to TSL through writing, teaching, service, and mentoring.
flag: Frequency reported inconsistently—some sources say at most every second year.

title: Frank M. Masters Transportation Engineering Award
eligibilityNote: ASCE member in good standing; best example of innovative/noteworthy planning, design, or construction of transportation facilities. Cannot be won more than once.

title: Wilbur S. Smith Award
eligibilityNote: ASCE Fellow or Member in good standing; special contributions enhancing the civil engineer's role in highway engineering.

title: James Laurie Prize
eligibilityNote: ASCE member in good standing; definitive contribution to the advancement of transportation engineering. Not awarded more than once to an individual.

title: IEEE ITSS Best PhD Dissertation Award
eligibilityNote: Best PhD dissertation in any ITS area; received PhD no more than 18 months before submission. No membership/nationality restriction. Presented at IEEE ITSC.

title: IEEE ITSS Young Researcher/Engineer Award
eligibilityNote: Early-career researcher/engineer recognized for contributions and leadership in ITS research/applications.

title: IEEE ITSS Outstanding Application Award
eligibilityNote: Significant contributions to applying ITS technologies in practical fields; open.

title: IEEE ITSS Lifetime Achievement Award
eligibilityNote: Exemplary contributions to Intelligent Transportation Systems over a career.

title: Jules Dupuit Prize
org: World Conference on Transport Research Society (WCTRS)
eligibilityNote: Person whose scientific and professional career has been of distinguished benefit to the transportation community.
prize: Presented at the World Conference on Transport Research
flag: Triennial WCTR conference (next: 2026).

title: WCTRS Prize for Best Paper by Young Authors
org: World Conference on Transport Research Society (WCTRS)
eligibilityNote: Best paper presented at the WCTR conference by a researcher under 35.
prize: Presented at the World Conference on Transport Research
flag: Tied to the triennial WCTR conference; requires conference paper.

title: Eric Pas Dissertation Prize
org: International Association for Travel Behaviour Research (IATBR)
eligibilityNote: Best PhD dissertation in travel behaviour research, approved by a university within the competition year. Membership not required; only the supervisor may nominate.
flag: Call for nominations distributed each January for the prior year's dissertations.

title: EASTS Best Paper Award / Yasoshima Prize
org: Eastern Asia Society for Transportation Studies (EASTS)
eligibilityNote: Best papers submitted to the biennial EASTS Conference; the Yasoshima Prize is the top honor among Best Paper recipients. Asia-focused society.
prize: Award certificate at the EASTS Conference

title: Theodore M. Matson Memorial Award
eligibilityNote: Outstanding contribution to traffic engineering through practical application, research, or advancement of the profession (est. 1957).
prize: Presented at the ITE Annual Meeting
flag: Nomination period opens January 1; deadlines vary by award.

title: ITE Young Member of the Year Award
eligibilityNote: Active ITE member aged 35 or younger on January 1 of the award year; from ITE's Young Leaders to Follow. Open to practitioners and academics.
prize: Recognition as ITE Young Member of the Year
flag: Young Leaders to Follow nominations close ~March 15 each year.

title: KST Academic Award (Paper Category)
eligibilityNote: Best-paper award for articles published in the KST journal (대한교통학회지). Announced at the annual general meeting in February. Since 1992. Separate 저술부문 also exists.

title: IEEE Intelligent Vehicles Symposium (IV) Best Paper Award
eligibilityNote: All papers accepted and presented at IEEE IV are eligible; a Best Student Paper Award requires a student first author enrolled at submission.
flag: Committee-selected. Held annually ~June (2026: Jun 22-25, Detroit).

title: IEEE ITSC Best Paper Award
eligibilityNote: All papers accepted and presented at ITSC are eligible; a Best Student Paper Award requires a student first author.
flag: Committee-selected. Held annually Sept/Oct (2026: Sept 15-18, Naples).

title: George N. Saridis Best Transactions Paper Award (IEEE T-ITS)
eligibilityNote: Best regular and survey papers published in IEEE Transactions on ITS in preceding years; committee-selected (no self-nomination).
flag: Society-level journal paper award.

title: IEEE ICRA Best Conference Paper Award
eligibilityNote: Best paper accepted and presented at ICRA. Parallel Best Student Paper and category awards exist. Authors must submit/present; IEEE membership not required.
prize: Certificate and recognition at the ICRA awards ceremony
flag: Selected across ALL robotics topics, not AV-specific.

title: IEEE/RSJ IROS Best Conference Paper Award
eligibilityNote: Best overall paper presented at IROS, with parallel Best Student Paper and sponsored category awards. Authors must submit/present.
prize: Certificate and recognition at the IROS awards ceremony
flag: Selected across ALL robotics topics, not AV-specific. Landing page varies by year.

title: Waymo Open Dataset Motion Prediction Challenge
eligibilityNote: WOMD (100k+ segments, HD maps + agent tracks). Predict multi-modal 8s trajectories for up to 8 agents; scored by minADE, minFDE, miss rate, mAP. Public leaderboard.
flag: Run annually 2020-2025, ~May close, winners at CVPR WAD. No formal 2026 Challenge, but leaderboards active.

title: Waymo Open Sim Agents Challenge (WOSAC)
eligibilityNote: WOMD. Generate realistic closed-loop behavior for ALL agents in a scene (optionally via Waymax); scored by a composite realism metric plus map/collision and kinematic metrics. Public leaderboard.
flag: Since 2023, winners at CVPR WAD; 2025 edition closed May 22, 2025. Leaderboard remains open.

title: nuScenes Prediction Challenge
eligibilityNote: nuScenes (1000 urban scenes, full sensor suite + HD maps). Predict future vehicle trajectories; scored by minADE_k, minFDE_1, miss-rate. Submit via EvalAI (591); server open year-round. Also detection/tracking/segmentation/panoptic challenges.
flag: Rolling year-round server; winner rounds historically at CVPR/ICRA AD workshops.

title: nuPlan Planning Challenge
eligibilityNote: nuPlan (1500+ hours; largest planning benchmark). Motion planning in open-loop, non-reactive and reactive closed-loop modes; submit Docker to EvalAI (1856) against a hidden test set; 10Hz, 15s rollouts.
prize: 2023 edition: $5,000-10,000 across modes + Innovation Award + possible internships
flag: Flagship competition at CVPR 2023 (Jan 30-May 26, 2023). Dataset/devkit/leaderboard remain; no confirmed later formal edition.

title: Argoverse 1 Motion Forecasting Competition
eligibilityNote: Argoverse 1 (~324k scenarios, 5s obs). Forecast focal-agent future given past tracks and HD-map lane graph; scored by minADE, minFDE, miss rate. EvalAI (454).
flag: First run 2019, annually at CVPR WAD. Largely superseded by Argoverse 2.

title: Argoverse 2 Motion Forecasting Competition
eligibilityNote: Argoverse 2 (250k scenarios, six cities; 5s obs + 6s prediction, HD vector maps). Multi-agent forecasting; scored by minADE, minFDE, miss rate (2.0 m). EvalAI (1719).
flag: Annually at CVPR WAD; leaderboard open year-round.

title: Argoverse 2 Scenario Mining Challenge
eligibilityNote: Argoverse 2 Sensor Dataset with 10,000 natural-language queries. Retrieve safety-critical scenarios from queries, then localize/detect/track; scored by HOTA-Temporal. Two tracks. EvalAI (2662).
prize: $2,500 for 1st in each of two tracks + $2,500 Innovation Award (2026)
flag: 2026 edition at CVPR WAD; opened Feb 18, 2026, closed ~May 31, 2026. Recurs annually.

title: CARLA Autonomous Driving Challenge
eligibilityNote: CARLA AD Leaderboard (v2.1 since Mar 2025). Drive routes under varied weather/dense traffic without privileged info; scored by Driving Score (route completion x infraction penalty). Tracks: SENSORS, MAP.
flag: Formal competition did NOT run in 2025; no 2026 edition announced, but leaderboard v2.1 usable. Historically tied to NeurIPS/CVPR.

title: Autonomous Grand Challenge - NAVSIM v2 End-to-End Driving
eligibilityNote: NAVSIM v2 (built on nuPlan/OpenScene). Benchmark end-to-end driving policies in data-driven non-reactive sim with reactive background traffic + synthetic novel views; scored by EPDMS. Must keep results public + submit a technical report.
prize: AGC 2025 pool $100,000. NAVSIM v2 E2E: $3,000 Champion at CVPR 2025; $5,000 Innovation + $3,000 Champion at ICCV 2025
flag: 2025 edition. CVPR phase closed May 10, 2025; ICCV phase Sept 27, 2025. Recurs annually (~CVPR June + ICCV/ECCV phase); 2026 not confirmed.

title: CVPR Workshop on Autonomous Driving (WAD) - Benchmark Challenges
eligibilityNote: Annual umbrella workshop hosting multiple AV benchmark tracks. WAD 2026 (Jun 3, 2026, Denver): Argoverse 2 Scenario Mining, Argoverse 2 LiDAR Scene Flow, KITScenes Long-Tail E2E. Prior years also Waymo, Argoverse, nuPlan, Nexar crash-prediction.
prize: Varies by track (some carry sponsor cash awards)
flag: Tracks change yearly. Use as the annual hub; individual tracks listed separately.

title: Traffic4cast (NeurIPS Traffic Prediction Competition)
eligibilityNote: Predict short-term city traffic; 2019-2021 as GPS-probe raster 'movies', 2022 congestion class + travel time on the road graph from sparse loop-counter data. >10^12 GPS points across up to 10 cities. Results at NeurIPS.
prize: Per-edition cash (2022: EUR 5k/3k/2k per track; 2019: up to $10,000 + IARAI fellowships)
flag: Past series (NeurIPS 2019-2022); no edition since 2022, IARAI site offline. Reference/benchmark only.

title: Amazon Last-Mile Routing Research Challenge
org: Amazon + MIT Center for Transportation & Logistics
eligibilityNote: Build data-driven models sequencing last-mile delivery stops to match experienced-driver route quality. 6,000+ historical Amazon routes.
flag: Past edition (2021). Reference/benchmark only; dataset still used.

title: ITE Collegiate Traffic Bowl
eligibilityNote: Jeopardy-style quiz on transportation planning, engineering and ITE history; section/district tournaments feed a Grand Championship. Teams of up to 3 student ITE members (US & Canada).
flag: Annual since 2009; 2026 Grand Championship Jul 20, 2026, Detroit.

title: Barbados Traffic Analysis Challenge
eligibilityNote: Predict roundabout congestion ~5 min ahead from four traffic-camera video streams (Macro-F1 70% + Accuracy 30%). Open globally (teams up to 4); special prize for top Barbadian participant.
flag: Closed Jan 25, 2026. Reference only.

title: NYC Taxi Trip Duration (Kaggle)
eligibilityNote: Predict total taxi trip duration in NYC (RMSLE). 2016 NYC TLC yellow-cab records. Playground competition, free Kaggle account.
prize: Playground — 4 x $3,000 for top supporting datasets
flag: Past Playground competition (2017). Reference/benchmark only.

title: Bike Sharing Demand (Kaggle)
eligibilityNote: Forecast hourly bike-rental demand from usage + weather (RMSLE). Capital Bikeshare (DC) 2011-2012 with weather/calendar features.
flag: Past Knowledge/Playground competition (2014-15). Reference/benchmark only.

title: KDD Cup 2017 - Highway Tollgates Traffic Flow Prediction
eligibilityNote: Two sub-tasks: predict average travel time and predict traffic volume through highway tollgates. China highway tollgate records (Alibaba Tianchi). 3,547 teams.
flag: KDD Cup recurs annually but themes change; traffic-flow edition was 2017 only. Reference/benchmark only.

title: KDD Cup 2019 - Context-Aware Multi-Modal Transportation Recommendation
eligibilityNote: Recommend/rank multimodal travel plans (walk, bike, drive, transit) given preferences and spatiotemporal context. Real Baidu Maps trip/query data. Ran Apr-Jul 2019.
prize: $45,000 for the transportation track (>$100k across all tracks)
flag: KDD Cup recurs annually but themes change; transportation edition was 2019 only. Reference/benchmark only.

title: TRANSFOR - Transportation Forecasting Competition
org: TRB Committee AED50 with IEEE ITSS
eligibilityNote: Task varies by edition: 2019 short-term traffic-speed forecasting on DiDi GAIA data (Xi'an/Chengdu); 2022 detecting/forecasting VRU crossings from LiDAR (Chattanooga). Present at the TRB Annual Meeting.
flag: Held irregularly (2013, 2019, 2022) at TRB Annual Meeting. Next edition unannounced.

title: TRB Transit Data Challenge
eligibilityNote: Showcase innovative use of transit data (dashboards, performance measures, novel data collection) to improve public-transit decisions. Open to professionals, academics, students.
prize: Recognition + present at the TRB Annual Meeting (no cash)
flag: Annual at the January TRB Annual Meeting; organizers note the 2026 edition is on hold.

title: Grab AI for S.E.A. Challenge - Traffic Management
eligibilityNote: Forecast ride-booking demand T+1 to T+5 (15-min steps) on geohash-aggregated normalised historical demand for a SE Asian city. Grab booking-demand dataset.
prize: SGD 20,000 pool + full-time Grab offers to top ~50
flag: Past edition (2019). Reference only; also had CV and Safety tracks.

title: 2025 Accra Mobility Prediction Hackathon (Yango x Zindi)
eligibilityNote: Predict taxi ride time in Accra from trip and weather features (RMSE). Jan-2025 Yango trips. Ghanaian residents only (teams up to 4); separate university-student prize track.
flag: 2025 edition closed (Jul 5, 2025); a 2024 Accra edition also ran. Reference only. Region-restricted (Ghana).

title: Lusaka Mobility Prediction Hackathon (Yango x Zindi)
eligibilityNote: Predict average road speed in Lusaka every 15 minutes across the day (RMSE). Sep-2023 Yango traffic data. Participants based in Zambia (teams up to 4).
flag: One-day hackathon (Nov 4, 2023). Reference only. Region-restricted (Zambia).

title: NYC Taxi Fare Prediction (Kaggle)
eligibilityNote: Predict NYC taxi fare (incl. tolls) from pickup/dropoff coords, time and passenger count (RMSE). 55M+ trip rows. Playground competition, free Kaggle account.
flag: Past Playground competition (2018). Reference/benchmark only.

title: Dwight David Eisenhower Transportation Fellowship (DDETFP)
eligibilityNote: Enrolled full-time at a US institution in a transportation-related graduate program. Open to US citizens, PRs, and non-citizens enrolled at a US school with a valid visa. Binding constraint: US-institution enrollment.
prize: Up to $10,000/yr tuition + monthly stipend + up to $1,500 TRB travel
flag: Requires enrollment at a US institution — not accessible from a Korean university. Annual NOFO on Grants.gov.

title: Eno Future Leaders Development Conference (LDC) Fellowship
eligibilityNote: Current master's/doctoral students in transportation fields. Must be NOMINATED by a US college/university (up to 2 per university); self-nominations not accepted. International students eligible only if nominated by a US institution. Attend the full DC program.
prize: ~$4,000 program cost covered ($100 materials fee); one week in DC (20 fellows)
flag: Requires nomination by a US institution — generally not accessible from a Korean university. Conference Jun 1-4, 2026.

title: Lee Schipper Memorial Scholarship
org: VREF, WRI Ross Center & Kuehne Climate Center
eligibilityNote: Early-career/young scholars worldwide in efficient and sustainable transport and energy. 2026: 5 awards across categories incl. a sustainable-freight track.
flag: 2026 award structure should be confirmed in the official guidelines PDF; deadline is for the expression of interest.

title: WTS Foundation Helene M. Overly Memorial Graduate Scholarship
eligibilityNote: Women only, enrolled in a transportation-related graduate program. International students eligible. Membership NOT required (free for students). Apply through a WTS chapter (mostly US/Canada). National award $10,000.
flag: Women only; must route the application through a WTS chapter (mostly US/Canada). Chapter deadlines typically fall; winners in May.

title: NVIDIA Graduate Fellowship Program
eligibilityNote: PhD students past their first year in CS/CE/EE/system architecture or related; full-time during the fellowship year. International students eligible. Requires an in-person summer internship at an NVIDIA research office. Invites AV, robotics, AI work.
prize: Up to US$60,000 per award + paid summer internship
flag: Mandatory internship at a listed NVIDIA office (none in Korea). 2026-27 cycle closed Sep 15, 2025 — reopens ~Aug/Sep.

title: Qualcomm Innovation Fellowship (QIF) - South Korea
eligibilityNote: Full-time MS/PhD students at Korean universities, enrolled for the full 2026-2027 year; applicant must be first author of the submitted paper. Areas incl. Autonomous Driving & Physical AI. Directly relevant to a KAIST AV/ITS researcher.
prize: KRW 4,000,000 per selected paper (to the student's university) + Qualcomm mentorship
flag: Korea edition award is smaller than US/Europe QIF; confirm the current-year deadline (apply via Google Form).

title: Google PhD Fellowship Program (East Asia)
eligibilityNote: PhD students must be NOMINATED by an eligible university (no direct applications). East Asia eligible; most coursework completed at nomination. Areas incl. ML and Machine Perception relevant to AV.
prize: East Asia: US$10,000 for a 1-year fellowship + a Google Research mentor
flag: General CS/ML fellowship (not transportation-specific); university nomination required; East Asia award smaller than other regions.

title: ACRP Graduate Research Award Program (Aviation)
eligibilityNote: Full-time graduate students at accredited US/Canada institutions, faculty-supervised. Eligible: US/Canada citizens/PRs, or holders of an active US/Canada student visa throughout the award. Research paper on a public-sector aviation topic.
flag: Requires enrollment at a US or Canadian institution — not accessible from a Korean university; aviation-focused.

title: Marie Sklodowska-Curie Actions (MSCA) Postdoctoral Fellowships
eligibilityNote: PhD holders with <=8 years research experience post-PhD, any nationality. European PF: move to an EU/Associated host. Global PF: 1-2 years at a non-European country then a 1-year EU return. Mobility rule: not >12 months in the host country in the prior 36. Submitted with a host.
prize: Living, mobility and family allowances + research/training costs for 1-3 years
flag: Field-agnostic; requires a European host. A KAIST researcher would apply to move to an EU host (or via Global PF).

title: Humboldt Research Fellowship (Postdoc / Experienced)
eligibilityNote: Non-German researchers of any nationality. Postdoc track: PhD <=4 years before application (Experienced: <=12 years). Requires a host in Germany and a strong publication record.
prize: EUR 3,000/month for a 6-24 month stay in Germany + allowances
flag: Field-agnostic; requires a German host. Applications anytime; committees meet Mar, Jul, Nov.

title: EASTS International Cooperative Research Activity (ICRA) Grant
org: Eastern Asia Society for Transportation Studies (EASTS)
eligibilityNote: For International Research Groups registered with EASTS. Two types: ICRA-A (incubating) and ICRA-B (dissemination). Grantees hold a special session at the EASTS conference. Group-based, not an individual fellowship.
prize: Research grant; limited awards per cycle
flag: Group-based grant requiring an established EASTS group — not directly targetable by an individual newcomer.

title: Sejong Science Fellowship (NRF)
org: National Research Foundation of Korea (한국연구재단, NRF) / 과기정통부
eligibilityNote: All-field basic research grant for early-career researchers (transportation/AI eligible). Domestic track generally within ~7 years of PhD; also 국외연수/복귀·유치 tracks. 2026 domestic-track application was Dec 3-16, 2025 via IRIS.
flag: Apply through your institution's 산학협력단 and IRIS. Confirm current-year call dates on NRF/IRIS.

title: Amelia Earhart Fellowship
eligibilityNote: Women of any nationality in a full-time PhD in aerospace engineering or space sciences, past 1 year of the PhD. Postdocs ineligible. Usable at any accredited university worldwide.
flag: Women only and strictly aerospace/space — ground-transportation AV work out of scope. Next deadline ~Nov 15, 2026.

title: USDOT UTC Outstanding Student of the Year Award
org: US DOT / Council of University Transportation Centers (CUTC)
eligibilityNote: One student nominated by each participating US University Transportation Center (UTC), on research merit, academics, professionalism, leadership. Requires affiliation with a US UTC. Honored at the CUTC banquet during the TRB Annual Meeting.
prize: US$1,000 + certificate + travel to the TRB Annual Meeting
flag: Nomination by a US UTC only — not accessible from Korean institutions; a recognition award, not an open application.

title: Waymo Open Dataset
eligibilityNote: 3D detection, tracking, motion forecasting, sim agents & vision-based end-to-end driving; multi-LiDAR + multi-camera
flag: Google sign-in required; non-commercial Waymo Dataset License

title: nuScenes
eligibilityNote: 3D detection, tracking, LiDAR & panoptic segmentation, prediction; 360-degree LiDAR + camera + radar
flag: Free registration; non-commercial CC BY-NC-SA 4.0

title: nuPlan
eligibilityNote: Closed-loop & open-loop motion planning benchmark; 1200h ego + agent trajectories with HD maps
flag: Free registration; non-commercial license

title: Argoverse 1
eligibilityNote: 3D tracking & motion forecasting (324k scenarios); LiDAR + ring/stereo camera + HD maps
flag: Non-commercial CC BY-NC-SA 4.0

title: Argoverse 2
eligibilityNote: Sensor 3D detection/tracking, motion forecasting (250k), LiDAR self-supervision, map change; LiDAR + camera + HD maps
flag: Non-commercial CC BY-NC-SA 4.0

title: KITTI Vision Benchmark Suite
org: KIT & Toyota Technological Institute at Chicago
eligibilityNote: Stereo, optical/scene flow, depth, visual odometry, 2D/3D detection, tracking & segmentation; stereo camera + Velodyne LiDAR + GPS/IMU
flag: Non-commercial CC BY-NC-SA 3.0

title: ONCE (One millioN sCenEs)
eligibilityNote: 3D object detection with semi-/self-supervised learning (1M LiDAR scenes, 7M images); LiDAR + camera
flag: Registration; Huawei terms of use (non-commercial)

title: Cityscapes
eligibilityNote: Pixel/instance/panoptic semantic segmentation & 3D vehicle detection of urban street scenes; stereo video (camera)
flag: Registration; free for non-commercial use

title: BDD100K
eligibilityNote: 10 driving tasks: detection, semantic/instance segmentation, drivable area, lane, MOT/MOTS, tagging; 100k driving videos (camera)
flag: Registration to download; project homepage currently unreachable

title: METR-LA & PEMS-BAY Traffic Speed Datasets
eligibilityNote: Spatiotemporal traffic speed forecasting on road-sensor graphs (207 LA + 325 Bay Area sensors); multivariate time series

title: Caltrans PeMS (Performance Measurement System)
eligibilityNote: Real-time & historical freeway loop-detector flow/speed/occupancy (~40k detectors); source of PEMS03/04/07/08 benchmarks; time series
flag: Free account registration required

title: LargeST
eligibilityNote: Large-scale traffic flow forecasting (8,600 California sensors, 5 years; CA/GLA/GBA/SD subsets); time series

title: Traffic4cast Datasets
eligibilityNote: City-wide traffic map-movie forecasting & graph ETA from sparse probe/loop data; spatiotemporal raster + road graph
flag: IARAI closed; data via HERE / GitHub; mixed open licenses

title: NYC TLC Trip Record Data
eligibilityNote: Yellow/green taxi & FHV trip records (pickup/dropoff time, zone, distance, fare); tabular mobility-demand time series

title: highD
eligibilityNote: Naturalistic highway vehicle trajectories from drone (110,500 vehicles, 6 German locations); trajectories + maps
flag: Registration; free non-commercial only, commercial license available

title: inD
eligibilityNote: Naturalistic road-user (vehicle + VRU) trajectories at German urban intersections from drone; trajectories + OpenDRIVE/Lanelet2 maps
flag: Registration; free non-commercial only, commercial license available

title: rounD
eligibilityNote: Naturalistic road-user trajectories at German roundabouts from drone (~13,740 users); trajectories + OpenDRIVE/Lanelet2 maps
flag: Registration; free non-commercial only, commercial license available

title: exiD
eligibilityNote: Naturalistic trajectories at German highway entries/exits & merging from drone (~69,430 users); trajectories + OpenDRIVE/Lanelet2 maps
flag: Registration; free non-commercial only, commercial license available

title: NGSIM (Next Generation Simulation)
eligibilityNote: Detailed vehicle trajectories at 10 Hz (US-101, I-80, Lankershim, Peachtree); car-following & lane-change modeling
flag: US Government open data (public domain)

title: INTERACTION Dataset
eligibilityNote: Interactive multi-agent motion/trajectory prediction (roundabouts, intersections, merging; multiple countries) with HD semantic maps
flag: Registration; non-commercial research license

title: KST Conference Best Paper Award
eligibilityNote: Awarded to outstanding papers presented at KST academic conferences. Given at the conference closing ceremony; grad students who present are eligible. Since 1999.
prize: 상장/표창; awarded at Feb and Sep conferences
flag: Paper submission follows each conference call (spring ~Feb, autumn ~Sep); verify on KST notice.

title: KST Park Chang-ho Scholarship
eligibilityNote: Scholarship for transportation/urban-planning undergraduates and transport-focused graduate students. Awarded at the autumn conference (September). Since 2013.
flag: Application/nomination process not detailed publicly; verify with KST secretariat.

title: Korea ITS Society Undergraduate Paper Competition
org: Korea Institute of Intelligent Transport Systems (한국ITS학회)
eligibilityNote: Held at the KITS fall conference. First author must be an undergraduate; must register as a student member. Selection by written review + presentation video. 2025 deadline was Sep 19, 2025.
prize: 대상 100만원 / 최우수 각 50만원 / 우수 각 30만원 / 장려 각 20만원
flag: 2026 dates not yet posted; inferred from 2025 (Sep 19).

title: NRF Young Researcher Program (Basic Research)
org: National Research Foundation of Korea (한국연구재단, NRF) / 과기정통부
eligibilityNote: Individual basic-research grant for new/young researchers (newly appointed faculty / early-career PhD holders). Part of 개인기초연구사업; 2026 selections included ~1,770 신진연구 projects. All fields incl. transportation/AI.
flag: Two calls per year (~Jan, mid-year); eligibility limits and dates change annually — verify on NRF/IRIS.

title: NRF Postdoctoral Overseas Training Fellowship
org: National Research Foundation of Korea (한국연구재단, NRF)
eligibilityNote: For Korean-national PhD holders (PhD from a domestic university within ~5 years) to conduct research at an overseas institution. Applied via IRIS. 2026 call: Feb 11-24, 2026.
flag: Part of the annual 이공분야 학술연구지원사업 cycle (~February); confirm current-year dates on NRF/IRIS.

title: NRF Postdoctoral Domestic Training Fellowship
org: National Research Foundation of Korea (한국연구재단, NRF)
eligibilityNote: Supports postdoctoral research (1-3 years, min 3 months) at a domestic university/national research institute across all S&E fields incl. transportation/AI. ~150 projects. Applied via IRIS.
flag: Part of the annual 학문후속세대지원 cycle (~February); confirm current-year dates on NRF/IRIS.

title: Hyundai Motor Group Autonomous Driving Challenge
eligibilityNote: Korea's largest autonomous-driving competition for university-student teams (since 2010). 2025: two rounds (Round 1 March, finals Sep 30) in a virtual simulator mirroring K-City, focused on AI End-to-End driving. 2025 winner UNIST (finalists incl. KAIST).
flag: 2026 registration schedule not yet announced.

title: Autonomous Driving AI Challenge (MSIT)
org: Ministry of Science and ICT (과기정통부) / KADIF
eligibilityNote: AI competition on 3D object detection, camera semantic segmentation, and surrounding-vehicle trajectory prediction. 2025 (2nd): applications Aug 11-Sep 12, competition Sep 15-Oct 17, awards Nov 14. Apply via KADIF (kadif.kr).
flag: Prize amounts not stated in the notice; verify the current call at kadif.kr.

title: International University Student EV Autonomous Driving Competition
eligibilityNote: University-student competition combining hands-on autonomous-driving/AI training with a race. 4th edition held Jul 9-12, 2025 at Jeju Shinhwa World with the International e-Mobility Expo.
flag: Registration opens months ahead of the July event; confirm 2026 (5th) dates on the expo site.

title: A1 Autonomous Driving Challenge
org: Ministry of Trade, Industry and Energy (산업통상자원부) / KIMAC
eligibilityNote: Team-based autonomous-driving challenge organized by MOTIE and managed by KIMAC. 2026 edition announced with team recruitment and schedule. Contact KIMAC 053-670-7848.
flag: Eligibility/prize/2026 dates not on the landing page; verify via KIMAC recruitment notice.

title: Land & Transportation Data Utilization Competition
eligibilityNote: Big-data competition using national transportation data. Two tracks: (1) policy ideas and (2) product/service development. 2024 ran Apr 19-Jun 26, 2024. Register via the National Transportation Data Open Market.
flag: Prize amounts not detailed; KOTI also runs a rolling '교통연구' journal paper call.

title: Korea Expressway 'Future Highway' Idea & Paper Competition
eligibilityNote: For domestic university/graduate students (individual or team up to 5). Submit an idea (PPT <=20 pages) or paper on the future of highways. 3rd edition awarded May 2025. Submit via ex.co.kr (exvision@ex.co.kr).
flag: Prize structure varies by edition/track; confirm current-year call on ex.co.kr.

title: KAIA Land, Infrastructure & Transport Technology Fair Contests
org: Ministry of Land, Infrastructure and Transport (국토교통부) / KAIA
eligibilityNote: The national land/infrastructure/transport R&D fair (MOLIT, hosted by KAIA) runs public contests such as an AI Video Competition. 2026 fair: Jun 24-26, 2026 at COEX, Seoul.
flag: General AI-video/drawing contests (limited fit for research papers); KAIA's researcher channel is 국토교통 R&D 과제공고 at kaia.re.kr.

title: DACON Mobility & Autonomous-Driving AI Competitions
eligibilityNote: Korea's main data-science competition platform; regularly hosts mobility-relevant ML challenges (autonomous-driving sensor prediction, traffic-accident risk prediction, transport data analysis). Open registration; cash prizes vary.
flag: Platform aggregator — individual competitions open/close throughout the year; check the live list.

title: MIT CEE Rising Stars
eligibilityNote: Within ~1yr of PhD (or PhD from 2025+), non-faculty preferred. Research presentations + faculty-career panels at MIT.

title: CPS Rising Stars
eligibilityNote: Outstanding PhD students & postdocs in Cyber-Physical Systems (incl. AV/ITS) pursuing academic careers.
flag: 2026 cycle not yet announced; announced via cps-vo.org.
